title: Why Local Rendering Changes the Product Risk
audience: founders and product operators evaluating an automated video pipeline
objective: Show that local MP4 generation turns publishing into an integration problem instead of a product problem.
takeaways: Draft packages need enough structure to be reviewed without guesswork.
Rendering locally proves the creative system can produce actual assets.
Once MP4 quality is acceptable, distribution can be handled by platform integrations.

title: The Minimum Useful Video Factory
audience: builders moving from prototype automation to a repeatable content system
objective: Define the smallest pipeline that creates reviewable drafts and playable videos.
takeaways: A draft package should include script, storyboard, captions, metadata, and QA notes.
The render step should consume the package directly, not a separate hand-built file.
Validation should check both content completeness and MP4 existence.
